entities.name: ICPL 87
entities.description: A pigeonpea variety developed by pedigree selection, characterized by high yield, short duration, and disease tolerance.

entities.name: PEDIGREE SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding method used to develop the ICPL 87 variety.

entities.name: ICPX73052
entities.description: The parent cross used to develop ICPL 87.

entities.name: T21
entities.description: One of the parent lines used in the cross to create ICPX73052.

entities.name: JA277
entities.description: One of the parent lines used in the cross to create ICPX73052.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The location where trials of ICPL 87 were conducted.

entities.name: UPAS120
entities.description: The control pigeonpea variety used for comparison in trials.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced per unit area, with ICPL 87 giving 3700-5200 kg/ha.

entities.name: MATURITY
entities.description: The time from sowing to harvest, which for ICPL 87 is 110-130 days.

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: The vertical measurement of the plant, which for ICPL 87 is 80-90 cm.

entities.name: GROWTH HABIT
entities.description: The physical form of the plant, described as semispreading and determinate for ICPL 87.

entities.name: HIGH DENSITY CULTIVATION
entities.description: A farming practice involving 330,000 plants per hectare, suitable for ICPL 87.

entities.name: FUSARIUM UDUM WILT
entities.description: A fungal disease to which ICPL 87 is tolerant.

entities.name: HELICOVERPA ARMIGERA
entities.description: The pod borer pest that causes damage to pigeonpea plants.
